_doc: Fixtures (and optionally specific format variants) that are known to fail
the fold-ON cross-tier conformance step but pass the fold-OFF step.
Why this exists:
CI runs the conformance suite twice. The first run passes
--disable-constant-folding to every compiler so output stays byte-stable
against the checked-in expected-script.hex / expected-ir.json goldens.
The second run (RUNAR_DISABLE_CONSTANT_FOLDING=0) re-runs every fixture
with folding ENABLED and enforces cross-tier hex + ANF parity across
all 7 compiler tiers (it skips golden-file comparison because the goldens
were stamped fold-OFF). The fold-on run catches latent fold bugs that
the historical fold-off-only loop would have missed.
Contract for additions to this file:
- Each entry MUST carry a one-line `reason` string. No bare lists.
- Each entry SHOULD carry a `tracking` ref (issue link, commit, or
pointer to a follow-up plan). The reviewer needs to see how the
divergence will be resolved, not just hidden.
- Entries are { fixture, formats?, reason, tracking? }. When `formats`
is omitted, the entire fixture (every format variant) is skipped
under fold-on. When present, only those format variants are skipped.
- The fold-OFF run still exercises the entry, so allowlisting here
does not lose coverage of the canonical golden output.
